req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 2
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-088 defines three testable behaviours: (1) a valid publish with departments selected succeeds and notifies employees [positive/EP], (2) publishing with zero departments is rejected with HTTP 422 [negative/NEG], and (3) the idempotency guarantee under retry must hold [boundary/STC]. A fourth case covers the exact boundary of exactly one department selected, distinguishing it from the zero-department error partition [boundary/BVA]. Decision-table reasoning is embedded in the positive case which combines the multi-department selection condition with the notification assertion. Each test targets the /org-announcements/new screen via the HR-app API, uses an admin-scoped session fixture, and cleans up created resources via teardown helpers so reruns remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments_succeeds
preconditions: At least two departments exist in the org
No announcement with the same title exists
actions: POST /org-announcements/new with valid title, body, and two department IDs
Assert response body contains announcement ID and status=published
Assert notification records exist for employees in both departments
expected: Notification records created for all employees in selected departments
trace: R-WEB-088: admin publishes announcement to chosen departments
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments_succeeds(
    admin_session,
    two_department_ids,
    announcement_cleanup
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Q3 All-Hands",
        "body": "Details inside.",
        "department_ids": two_department_ids
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/org-announcements/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data
    assert data["status"] == "published"
    announcement_cleanup(data["id"])
    notifs = admin_session.get(
        f"/notifications?announcement_id={data['id']}"
    ).json()
    employee_ids = <get_employee_ids_for_departments>(two_department_ids)
    notified_ids = {n["employee_id"] for n in notifs}
    assert set(employee_ids) == notified_ids

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_no_departments_returns_422
actions: POST /org-announcements/new with valid title and body but empty department_ids list
Assert error body references the department_ids field
trace: R-WEB-088: publishing without any department selection shall be rejected
R-WEB-088: error pathway 422 when no departments selected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_with_no_departments_returns_422(
    admin_session
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Empty Dept Announcement",
        "body": "Should be rejected.",
        "department_ids": []
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/org-announcements/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    error_data = resp.json()
    error_fields = <extract_error_fields>(error_data)
    assert "department_ids" in error_fields
    search = admin_session.get(
        "/org-announcements?title=Empty+Dept+Announcement"
    ).json()
    assert len(search.get("items", [])) == 0

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department_succeeds
preconditions: At least one department exists in the org
No announcement with the same title exists
actions: POST /org-announcements/new with exactly one department ID
Assert announcement is published and employees in that single department are notified
expected: Notification records created only for employees in the single selected department
trace: R-WEB-088: boundary between zero-department rejection and valid single-department publish
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department_succeeds(
    admin_session,
    single_department_id,
    announcement_cleanup
):
    payload = {
        "title": "One-Dept Announcement",
        "body": "Single department boundary test.",
        "department_ids": [single_department_id]
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/org-announcements/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "published"
    announcement_cleanup(data["id"])
    notifs = admin_session.get(
        f"/notifications?announcement_id={data['id']}"
    ).json()
    expected_emp_ids = <get_employee_ids_for_departments>([single_department_id])
    notified_ids = {n["employee_id"] for n in notifs}
    assert set(expected_emp_ids) == notified_ids

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_is_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: At least one department exists in the org
A unique idempotency key is prepared for this test run
actions: POST /org-announcements/new with valid payload and idempotency key header
Assert HTTP 201 is returned and record the announcement ID
POST the identical payload with the same idempotency key a second time
Assert the second response is also 2xx and returns the same announcement ID
Assert only one announcement record exists for the given title
expected: First POST returns HTTP 201 with a stable announcement ID
Second POST returns HTTP 200 or 201 with the identical announcement ID
Exactly one announcement record exists (no duplicate created)
trace: R-WEB-088: the action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_publish_announcement_is_idempotent_under_retry(
    admin_session,
    single_department_id,
    announcement_cleanup
):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "title": f"Idempotent-{idem_key[:8]}",
        "body": "Retry safety test.",
        "department_ids": [single_department_id]
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp1 = admin_session.post(
        "/org-announcements/new", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    first_id = resp1.json()["id"]
    announcement_cleanup(first_id)
    resp2 = admin_session.post(
        "/org-announcements/new", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert resp2.json()["id"] == first_id
    search = admin_session.get(
        f"/org-announcements?title={payload['title']}"
    ).json()
    assert len(search.get("items", [])) == 1
